canonical.title: Warm October 2026 in Europe: where to go from Belgium

sources.title: https://weatherspark.com/m/150427/10/Average-Weather-in-October-in-Tenerife-Spain
sources.claim: Tenerife October daily highs decrease from 27°C (81°F) to 26°C (78°F) across the month with cloud cover rising from 28% to 38%.

sources.title: https://www.holiday-weather.com/tenerife/averages/october/
sources.claim: Tenerife averages 25°C daytime highs in October with about 10 mm of rain over ~4 days and 7-8 sunshine hours.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/canary-islands/tenerife
sources.claim: Tenerife sea temperature peaks at 23-24°C from August to October, warm enough for swimming.

sources.title: https://www.holiday-weather.com/gran_canaria/averages/october/
sources.claim: Gran Canaria October averages 24°C high, 20°C low with stable temperatures and ~11 hours daily sunlight.

sources.title: https://www.weather2travel.com/gran-canaria/october/
sources.claim: Gran Canaria October sea temperature averages 22°C, the year's warmest, with only ~13 mm rain over ~9 days.

sources.title: https://www.holiday-weather.com/lanzarote/averages/october/
sources.claim: Lanzarote October highs drop from 27°C to 25°C, with ~11 mm rain over 4 days, 9 sunshine hours and 22°C sea.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/canary-islands/fuerteventura
sources.claim: Fuerteventura October averages 25°C daytime with sea around 22.5°C; regional highs 24-26°C.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/madeira
sources.claim: Funchal Madeira October averages 24.5°C highs early month falling to 22.9°C late, with sea cooling 23°C to 22.2°C.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/malta
sources.claim: Malta October sees high rainfall with 85+ mm/month and the wet season starts; daytime highs around 24°C.

sources.title: https://weatherspark.com/m/150260/10/Average-Weather-in-October-in-Malta
sources.claim: Malta October daily highs decline from 26°C (79°F) early month to 22°C (72°F) by month-end - borderline for ≥20°C late October.

sources.title: https://www.holiday-weather.com/paphos/averages/october/
sources.claim: Paphos Cyprus October averages 27°C daytime high, 16°C night, 18 mm rain over 5 days, sea 24.5°C.

sources.title: https://www.thomascook.com/holidays/weather/cyprus/october/
sources.claim: Cyprus in October has ~10 hours daily sunshine and sea temps still above 20°C, suitable for swimming.

sources.title: https://www.greeka.com/crete/heraklion/weather/
sources.claim: Heraklion Crete October averages 24.3°C high, 17.8°C low, 95 mm rain, sea 23°C, 6 sunshine hours.

sources.title: https://www.greeka.com/crete/chania/weather/
sources.claim: Chania Crete October averages 23.9°C high, 16.8°C low - borderline late October as cooler weather sets in.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/greece/rhodes
sources.claim: Rhodes October starts warm at 25-27°C early then milder; autumn becomes rainier as month progresses.

sources.title: https://www.holiday-weather.com/rhodes/averages/october
sources.claim: Rhodes October averages 24°C max, 17°C min, 68 mm rain over 5 days, sea 23°C, 8 sunshine hours.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/greece/kos
sources.claim: Kos October averages 26°C high, 18°C low, ~59 mm rain over 6 days, sea 23°C - autumn becomes milder/rainier through month.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/italy/catania
sources.claim: Catania Sicily October is the wettest month with 105-113 mm rain, average highs 23°C, 7 sunshine hours.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/italy/palermo
sources.claim: Palermo October daily high 24.3°C, low 17.5°C, 80 mm rain over 8 days, sea 22.4°C, 7 sunshine hours.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/portugal/faro
sources.claim: Faro Algarve October highs drop from 25°C to 22°C across the month, ~70 mm rain over ~8 days, sea ~19°C.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/spain/malaga
sources.claim: Málaga October highs 24-25°C with first half particularly warm/sunny; ~45-57 mm rain over ~5 days.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/spain/seville
sources.claim: Seville October highs drop from 29°C to 24°C across the month, with ~72 mm rain over 10 days; first rains begin.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/greece/athens
sources.claim: Athens October averages 23.8°C high, 15.9°C low, 45 mm rain over 5 days, sea 22°C, 6.5 sunshine hours - borderline late October.

sources.title: https://www.holiday-weather.com/peloponnese/averages/
sources.claim: Peloponnese October temperatures range 20-25°C, comfortable conditions across the region.

sources.title: https://www.weather2travel.com/algarve/faro/october/
sources.claim: Faro October averages 22°C daytime, 17°C night, with sea temperature around 19°C - borderline for ≥20°C late October.

sources.title: https://www.travelwires.com/brussels-airport-expands-to-180-destinations-for-summer-2026
sources.claim: Brussels Airport (BRU) will offer direct flights to 180 destinations in 2026 with 70 airlines, season starting March 29.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-crl-to-tfs
sources.claim: Ryanair operates 10 weekly flights from Charleroi (CRL) to Tenerife South (TFS) with average flight time 4h30, sole operator on the route.

sources.title: https://www.brussels-charleroi-airport.com/en/destinations
sources.claim: Charleroi (CRL) has Ryanair direct service to Tenerife, Gran Canaria, Lanzarote, Fuerteventura, Madeira, Faro, Paphos, Heraklion, Chania, Rhodes, Athens, Catania, Palermo, Málaga and Sevilla; Athens also served by Pegasus.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-bru-to-lca
sources.claim: Aegean is the only carrier flying nonstop from Brussels (BRU) to Larnaca (LCA), with 5 weekly flights as of January 2026 and a flight time of around 4 hours.

sources.title: https://www.brusselsairlines.com/lhg/be/en/o-d/cy-cy/brussels-heraklion-crete
sources.claim: Brussels Airlines operates direct flights from BRU to Heraklion (HER), Crete.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-bru-to-her
sources.claim: Brussels Airlines operates 8 weekly nonstop flights from BRU to Heraklion with average flight time 3h20 (April 2026).

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-bru-to-fnc
sources.claim: BRU to Funchal (Madeira) has 3 weekly nonstop flights with average duration 4h30; Brussels Airlines and TUI Fly are the operators.

sources.title: https://www.flightsfrom.com/BRU-CTA
sources.claim: Brussels Airlines and TUI Fly fly direct from BRU to Catania (CTA), seasonal April–November.

sources.title: https://www.flightsfrom.com/BRU-PFO
sources.claim: TUI fly Belgium operates 2 weekly nonstop flights from BRU to Paphos (PFO) with flight time 4h25; Ryanair also serves the route.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-crl-to-mla
sources.claim: Ryanair is the sole nonstop carrier from Charleroi (CRL) to Malta (MLA), operating 9 weekly flights with departures spread 07:05–21:50.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-crl-to-agp
sources.claim: Ryanair flies CRL to Málaga (AGP) 11 times weekly (about 2 daily), sole operator on the route.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-crl-to-fao
sources.claim: CRL to Faro (FAO) drops to 1 weekly Ryanair flight by March 2026 (off-season), sole operator.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-bru-to-tfs
sources.claim: BRU to Tenerife South (TFS) has 11 weekly nonstop flights with average flight time 5 hours; Brussels Airlines, Transavia and TUI Fly all operate the route.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-ath-to-bru
sources.claim: BRU–Athens has ~25–26 weekly direct flights operated by Brussels Airlines, Aegean and SAS, with flight time approximately 3h20.

sources.title: https://www.flightsfrom.com/BRU-AGP
sources.claim: Brussels Airlines runs 9 weekly nonstop flights BRU–Málaga (AGP) with flight duration 2h55; Vueling also serves the route.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-fao-to-bru
sources.claim: Brussels Airlines flies BRU–Faro (FAO) with 6 weekly nonstop services.

sources.title: https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-bru-to-chq
sources.claim: Brussels Airlines operates ~2 weekly direct flights from BRU to Chania (CHQ) with flight time 3h25.

sources.title: https://www.brusselsairlines.com/lhg/be/en/dest/c/greece
sources.claim: Brussels Airlines flies from BRU direct to Athens, Heraklion, Chania, Kos and Rhodes.

sources.title: https://www.flightsfrom.com/MLA-CRL
sources.claim: Ryanair operates the CRL–Malta route with flight time 2h55.

sources.title: https://www.brusselsairlines.com/lhg/be/en/o-d/cy-cy/brussels-malaga
sources.claim: Brussels Airlines confirms a direct service from BRU to Málaga.

sources.title: https://www.vueling.com/en/flights-from-brussels-to-malaga
sources.claim: Vueling operates direct flights from Brussels to Málaga.

sources.title: https://www.google.com/travel/flights/flights-from-brussels-to-seville.html
sources.claim: Transavia operates ~4 weekly nonstop flights BRU to Seville (SVQ); Brussels Airlines does not serve this route directly.

sources.title: https://www.volcanoteide.com/en
sources.claim: Tenerife's signature attraction is Mount Teide, Spain's highest peak at 3,718 m, accessible by cable car with day, sunset, or stargazing tours; Teide National Park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the most visited national park in Europe.

sources.title: https://www.saltinourhair.com/spain/tenerife-itinerary/
sources.claim: A typical week in Tenerife splits between north (La Laguna, Anaga laurel forests, Puerto de la Cruz) and south (resorts, Siam Park, Los Gigantes cliffs), with central Teide as a day trip — combining volcano, forests, towns, and beaches.

sources.title: https://www.oliverstravels.com/blog/which-canary-island-is-right-for-you/
sources.claim: Tenerife is the most varied and lively Canary, known for all-inclusive resorts, beaches and clubbing, drawing 10+ million annual visitors; Gran Canaria feels like a 'little continent' with dramatic terrain variation appealing to adventure seekers.

sources.title: https://www.hellocanaryislands.com/nature-spaces/gran-canaria/maspalomas-dunes-nature-reserve/
sources.claim: Gran Canaria's signature sight is the 404-hectare Maspalomas Dunes Nature Reserve, a Sahara-like dune system with beach, palm grove and brackish La Charca lagoon hosting up to 24 migratory bird species; camel rides and the Costa Canaria promenade are key activities.

sources.title: https://www.livingtours.com/en/blog/main-atractions-gran-canaria-spain.html
sources.claim: Gran Canaria pairs beach/nightlife in Maspalomas-Playa del Inglés with mountain villages like Teror and lush hill attractions like Palmitos Park, plus the Las Palmas capital — fast contrasts in one week.

sources.title: https://www.getyourguide.com/timanfaya-national-park-l36127/
sources.claim: Lanzarote's headline attraction is Timanfaya National Park — a Mars-like landscape of 100+ volcanoes featuring a coach-only Lunar Route, geyser/fire demonstrations, and the El Diablo restaurant where food is grilled by geothermal heat.

sources.title: https://www.machupicchu.org/lanzarote-travel-guide-2026-timanfaya-cesar-manrique-biosphere-reserve.htm
sources.claim: Lanzarote is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve where César Manrique's art-architecture (Jameos del Agua, Mirador del Río, Cactus Garden) is fused with the volcanic landscape; a classic day pairs Timanfaya morning with La Geria volcanic-vineyard lunch.

sources.title: https://beachverdict.com/guides/best-beaches-in-fuerteventura
sources.claim: Fuerteventura's defining feature is empty beaches and world-class windsports: Sotavento has 9 km of sand with flat waist-deep water and consistent side-shore wind, drawing half of Europe's kitesurfers; Cofete is a remote wild Atlantic beach.

sources.title: https://www.timeout.com/spain/things-to-do/best-things-to-do-in-fuerteventura
sources.claim: Fuerteventura highlights include Corralejo Natural Park dunes, Isla de Lobos day trip (entry pass required, keeps crowds down), El Cotillo surf, and Oasis Park zoo/botanical garden — quieter and less developed than Tenerife or Gran Canaria.

sources.title: https://visitmadeira.com/en/what-to-do/nature-seekers/notice-to-walkers-and-list-of-walks/
sources.claim: Madeira's signature activity is the levada walks — irrigation-channel trails through Laurisilva forest; from January 2026 mandatory pre-booking is required for popular PR trails via the SIMplifica platform.

sources.title: https://beyondmadeira.com/best-levada-walks/
sources.claim: Top Madeira hikes include Levada do Caldeirão Verde (PR9) ending at a waterfall, 25 Fontes & Risco at Rabaçal, Vereda dos Balcões (PR11), and the Ponta de São Lourenço peninsula (PR8); September–November is recommended as ideal hiking season.

sources.title: https://www.packthesuitcases.com/how-to-spend-a-week-in-madeira-a-7-day-itinerary-without-a-car/
sources.claim: A week in Madeira typically combines Funchal city base (markets, cable car to Monte, toboggan ride) with day trips to Pico Ruivo summit, Caldeirão Verde, Câmara de Lobos and the western Rabaçal/Porto Moniz natural pools; pace is slow and nature-focused.

sources.title: https://www.packthesuitcases.com/things-to-do-in-valletta-malta/
sources.claim: Malta's capital Valletta packs St John's Co-Cathedral (Caravaggio), Upper Barrakka Gardens above Grand Harbour, Fort St Elmo, and Casa Rocca Piccola into a tiny, walkable UNESCO peninsula; cruise crowds arrive mid-morning so go early.

sources.title: https://www.alwayspacktissues.com/post/things-to-do-in-mdina-malta
sources.claim: Mdina, the 'Silent City', is a fortified medieval hilltop former capital of narrow alleys and Baroque palaces, best visited at sunset when light hits the limestone and day-tour crowds leave; pairs naturally with Rabat catacombs.

sources.title: https://www.grumpycamel.com/things-to-do-in-malta/
sources.claim: A typical Malta week covers Valletta, Mdina/Rabat, Marsaxlokk Sunday fish market, the Three Cities, Blue Grotto, prehistoric Ħaġar Qim/Mnajdra temples (older than Stonehenge) and a Gozo day trip via 25-min ferry from Cirkewwa.

sources.title: https://thetravelbunny.com/things-to-do-in-paphos-cyprus/
sources.claim: Paphos signature sights are the UNESCO Kato Paphos Archaeological Park (Roman mosaics) and Tombs of the Kings, plus Avakas Gorge hike, Aphrodite nature trail, and Blue Lagoon day trips; harbour promenade is the evening focal point.

sources.title: https://wanderingourworld.com/larnaca-vs-paphos/
sources.claim: Larnaca is more compact, beach-and-bar focused with palm-lined Finikoudes promenade and easy reach of Ayia Napa nightlife (30 min); Paphos has more historic sites and outdoor activities but weaker beaches — the two are 1.5 hours apart by car.

sources.title: https://www.greeka.com/crete/chania/
sources.claim: Chania (western Crete) is widely considered the most beautiful city in Crete with a Venetian harbour, Ottoman mosque, and old town; it's the base for Samaria Gorge (open May–October), Elafonisi pink-sand beach, Falassarna and Balos.

sources.title: https://rtwin30days.com/crete-greece-5-perfect-days/
sources.claim: Heraklion suits travellers prioritising Knossos Palace and the Archaeological Museum, and is better positioned to reach central/eastern Crete (Lassithi, Phaistos, Matala); Chania is more touristy/picturesque while Heraklion feels more authentic and working-Greek.

sources.title: https://www.discovergreece.com/travel-ideas/best-of/15-best-things-do-chania
sources.claim: A week-long Chania-base itinerary covers Old Town, Samaria Gorge full-day hike, Elafonisi/Falassarna beach days, Akrotiri peninsula monasteries, Loutro south-coast village, and Rethymno old town day trip.

sources.title: https://hostelgeeks.com/21-things-to-do-rhodes/
sources.claim: Rhodes' signature is the UNESCO Medieval Old Town — the oldest inhabited medieval city in Europe — with the Street of the Knights, Palace of the Grand Master, and pedestrian-only walls; Lindos village + acropolis and St Paul's Bay are the main south-island draw.

sources.title: https://www.lelongweekend.com/rhodes-to-symi-day-trip/
sources.claim: A standard Rhodes week adds a Symi catamaran day trip (1 hr each way, May 16–Oct 31 daily), Tsambika and Anthony Quinn Bay beaches, Valley of the Butterflies, and the Acropolis of Lindos; October offers 20–25°C and lighter crowds.

sources.title: https://thingstodoinsicily.com/month/october/
sources.claim: Sicily in October centres on Mount Etna (grape harvest at 700 m, summit fumaroles), Valley of the Temples, Palermo street food, Trapani salt pans with flamingos, and Syracuse/Ortigia; daily highs around 25°C with quieter sites but ferry sailings to Aeolians/Egadi shrink mid-month.

sources.title: https://www.thesmoothescape.com/the-perfect-one-week-sicily-itinerary/
sources.claim: A classic Sicily week hits Palermo, San Vito Lo Capo, Agrigento, Ragusa Ibla/Ortigia, Taormina (cliff-top with ancient theatre views to Etna), Mount Etna, and Cefalù — busy day-tripping pace with significant driving between regions.

sources.title: https://www.earthtrekkers.com/best-things-to-do-in-algarve/
sources.claim: Algarve highlights are Ponta da Piedade cliffs/sea caves (boat or kayak), Praia da Marinha, Benagil cave, Lagos Old Town, Cape St Vincent, and Ria Formosa lagoon near Faro; pace is slow-beach with golf and hiking add-ons.

sources.title: https://www.roughguides.com/articles/algarve-weather-october-travel-tips/
sources.claim: October in the Algarve is shoulder-season with ~23°C highs, sea around 19°C, lush green landscapes, up-to-30% lodging discounts, autumn bird migration, and Faro's Feira de Santa Iria; quieter beaches and ideal for hiking, cycling, and golf.

sources.title: https://www.tripadvisor.ie/Attractions-g187438-Activities-Malaga_Costa_del_Sol_Province_of_Malaga_Andalucia.html
sources.claim: Málaga's top sights are the Alcazaba Moorish fortress, Picasso Museum, Gibralfaro castle for sunset, Cathedral, and 40+ museums; signature experiences include espetos (sardine skewers) on the beach, flamenco, and the Caminito del Rey clifftop walk in the province.

sources.title: https://www.visitcostadelsol.com/
sources.claim: Málaga/Costa del Sol pairs city culture with Costa del Sol beaches (Marbella, Nerja, Torremolinos) and inland white villages like Mijas and Ronda; October is part of the recommended September–November shoulder window with mild weather and uncrowded beaches.

sources.title: https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowTopic-g190372-i9179-k13650146-Paphos_or_Larnaca-Cyprus.html
sources.claim: Forum consensus: Larnaca is preferred by travellers wanting compact walkable beach-bar atmosphere with good food; Paphos is preferred for sightseeing and a more 'tourist-resort' setup with better hotel quality but weaker beaches.

sources.title: https://drifterplanet.com/is-malta-worth-visiting/
sources.claim: Reviewer consensus: Malta is worth a full week — small enough to see comprehensively, big on history with megalithic temples, walled cities, and a Gozo day trip; rental car strongly recommended for flexibility.

sources.title: https://www.expedia.com/lp/flights/bru/tfs/brussels-to-tenerife
sources.claim: Brussels–Tenerife flights start around $147 round trip, with TUI fly, Transavia and Brussels Airlines as primary direct operators.

sources.title: https://radicalstorage.com/travel/is-tenerife-expensive/
sources.claim: Tenerife mid-range restaurant meal for two with drinks runs €35–60; menu del día €9–13.

sources.title: https://www.budgetyourtrip.com/hotels/spain/tenerife-2510529
sources.claim: Average mid-range hotel in Tenerife costs about $84 per night, weekly average ~$580 excluding tax.

sources.title: https://www.discovercars.com/spain-canary-islands/tenerife
sources.claim: Car rental in Tenerife runs roughly €300–450 per week for a small/mid-sized car.

sources.title: https://www.skyscanner.net/routes/brus/fnc/brussels-to-madeira.html
sources.claim: Brussels–Madeira (Funchal) round-trip flights start from around £76, with Ryanair from Charleroi the cheapest direct option.

sources.title: https://www.fly4free.com/flight-deals/europe/cheap-flights-to-madeira-4-3/
sources.claim: Ryanair Brussels Charleroi to Madeira can be found from around €56 round trip in shoulder season.

sources.title: https://www.portugalinvestmentproperties.com/cost-of-living-in-madeira/
sources.claim: Funchal mid-range dining is cheap by Portuguese standards (~26% lower than Lisbon); single-person living cost ~€1,200/mo signals reasonable food prices.

sources.title: https://beyondmadeira.com/madeira-hiking-fee-4-5-euros/
sources.claim: Madeira's signature levada trails now charge €4.50 per person (€10.50 for Pico do Areeiro–Pico Ruivo) from 2026.

sources.title: https://www.skyscanner.net/routes/brus/mt/brussels-to-malta.html
sources.claim: Brussels–Malta direct round-trip fares start around £29 with Ryanair, Brussels Airlines and KM Malta operating the route.

sources.title: https://www.myrent.mt/guides/cost-of-living-malta
sources.claim: Malta mid-range restaurant dinner costs €25–40 per person; Valletta is one of the more expensive areas with rents €1,100–1,600/mo.

sources.title: https://www.brusselsairlines.com/lhg/be/en/o-d/cy-cy/brussels-larnaca-cyprus
sources.claim: Brussels Airlines lists Brussels–Larnaca starting around €322; typical RT averages near €700 making Cyprus the most expensive flight on the shortlist.

sources.title: https://www.numbeo.com/cost-of-living/in/Paphos
sources.claim: Cyprus mid-range hotels (2–3 stars) run $90–175 per night double, with Paphos cheaper than Limassol.

sources.title: https://www.skyscanner.com/routes/brus/her/brussels-to-crete-heraklion.html
sources.claim: Brussels–Heraklion round-trip from $187, with TUI fly direct service averaging $207; October Ryanair fares from $59 one-way.

sources.title: https://www.machupicchu.org/rhodes-budget-guide-2026-complete-cost-breakdown.htm
sources.claim: Rhodes mid-range hotel runs €70–130 per night, week of accommodation €490–770, mid-range food €35–50/day per person.

sources.title: https://www.omio.com/flights/brussels/rhodes-zbfek
sources.claim: Brussels–Rhodes Ryanair direct from Charleroi runs April–October, fares from ~€122 round trip; October is among cheapest months.

sources.title: https://wavydestination.com/cost-of-living-in-sicily-2026-real-monthly-expenses-rent-food-budget-guide/
sources.claim: Sicily three-star hotels run €60–95/night in shoulder season; mid-range dining €60–90/day per person.

sources.title: https://www.momondo.com/flights/brussels/palermo
sources.claim: Brussels–Sicily (Palermo/Catania) round-trip fares typically $98–200; Lufthansa from €146 to Catania.

sources.title: https://www.kayak.com/flight-routes/Brussels-Bruxelles-National-BRU/Faro-FAO
sources.claim: Brussels–Faro round-trip fares start as low as $51, with Ryanair, Brussels Airlines, Transavia and TAP all serving the route.

sources.title: https://www.idealista.pt/en/news/financial-advice-in-portugal/2026/01/19/64552-the-cost-of-living-in-the-algarve-a-comprehensive-guide
sources.claim: Algarve mid-range three-course meal for two ~€40, inexpensive main €12; cost of living moderate among Portuguese regions.

sources.title: https://www.kayak.com/flight-routes/Brussels-Bruxelles-National-BRU/Malaga-AGP
sources.claim: Brussels–Málaga round-trip from $56 on Ryanair/Brussels Airlines; 4 weekly direct frequencies, ~2h50 flight.

sources.title: https://publicholidays.be/school-holidays/
sources.claim: Belgian autumn (Toussaint) school break runs 19 Oct–1 Nov 2026 (Flemish/German) and 2–7 Nov 2026 (French) — these dates spike flight prices ex-Belgium.

sources.title: https://www.machupicchu.org/canary-islands-budget-guide-2026-complete-cost-breakdown.htm
sources.claim: Lanzarote/Fuerteventura mid-range accommodation runs €60–100/night; mid-range Canaries 7-day couple budget €1,800–2,800 excl flights.

sources.title: https://lanzarotetransfer.com/blog/how-much-money-do-you-need-for-a-trip-to-lanzarote/
sources.claim: Lanzarote 7-day couple trip total typically lands €1,000–2,500 depending on style.

sources.title: https://www.numbeo.com/cost-of-living/in/Heraklion
sources.claim: Heraklion city bus single ride ~€2; modest local transport budget for Crete with car rental optional.

sources.title: https://www.belgieschoolvakanties.be/2026/herfstvakantie/
sources.claim: Belgian (Vlaanderen) autumn school holiday in 2026 runs Monday 2 Nov to Sunday 8 Nov, meaning the last week of October (26-30 Oct) is NOT a Belgian school break.

sources.title: https://www.timeanddate.com/news/time/europe-starts-dst-2026.html
sources.claim: European DST ends Sunday 25 October 2026, clocks go back one hour, making evenings noticeably shorter for the rest of the trip window.

sources.title: https://calimacanarias.com/en/alerts/
sources.claim: Calima (Saharan dust) events in the Canary Islands trigger official AEMET alerts, can dramatically reduce visibility and air quality, and disrupt flights.

sources.title: https://euroweeklynews.com/2026/04/01/calima-dust-storm-causes-air-travel-delays-in-canary-islands/
sources.claim: Calima episodes regularly disrupt air travel into the Canary Islands; an April 2026 event caused widespread flight delays.

sources.title: https://www.worlddata.info/europe/portugal/hurricanes.php
sources.claim: Madeira and the Azores have been hit by hurricanes between June and November, with the highest risk in September and October.

sources.title: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hurricane_Leslie_(2018)
sources.claim: Hurricane Leslie (October 2018) prompted a tropical storm warning for Madeira and passed 325 km north-northwest of the island before hitting Portugal — illustrative of the late-October ex-tropical risk.

sources.title: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_Spanish_floods
sources.claim: On 29-30 October 2024 a DANA / gota fría dropped >300 mm of rain in 4 hours over Valencia, killing hundreds — late October is peak gota fría season for eastern Spain.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/greece/crete
sources.claim: Crete's autumn officially starts in October with the first rains; rain becomes more likely especially in the second half of the month.

sources.title: https://www.worldwidegreeks.com/threads/when-does-tourist-season-end-in-greece.3997/
sources.claim: Many Greek-island resort hotels and beach clubs (e.g. parts of Faliraki on Rhodes, Kos resorts like Lambi/Kardamena) wind down mid-October and have shut by 1 November, while main towns (Rhodes Town, Heraklion, Chania) stay open year-round.

sources.title: https://www.climatestotravel.com/climate/italy/sicily
sources.claim: October is one of Sicily's wettest months with intense showers and sirocco-driven storms; sirocco gusts can reach 100 km/h in autumn.

sources.title: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sirocco
sources.claim: Sirocco winds reach up to 100 km/h, are most common in autumn and spring, and bring storms and 'blood rain' (red Saharan dust mixed with rain) to southern Italy and Malta.

sources.title: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Climate_of_Malta
sources.claim: Malta's October sees the first proper autumn storms with ~90 mm rain over ~9 days; the gregale wind can hit 90 km/h during these storms.

sources.title: https://www.cyprusparadise.com/blog/whats-the-weather-like-in-cyprus-in-october/
sources.claim: Cyprus in October stays the warmest of the shortlist (25-29 °C, only 2-3 rainy days, ~18 mm in Paphos), the most 'set and forget' weather pick.

sources.title: https://www.earthtrekkers.com/algarve-in-october/
sources.claim: The Algarve stays open all season in October — restaurants, beaches, boat tours all running — with ~80 mm rain over ~10 days, increasing toward month-end.

sources.title: https://beyondmadeira.com/weather-by-month/
sources.claim: Madeira in October stays at 20-25 °C with stable trail conditions early in the month, increasing rain risk toward November.

sources.title: https://canarias-lovers.com/en/visiting-tenerife-october-weather-temperatures/
sources.claim: Tenerife in October draws growing crowds of northern-European wintering visitors, so prices and bookings rise even though weather stays warm.

sources.title: https://www.rmets.org/metmatters/cut-lows-cold-drops-and-dana
sources.claim: DANA / gota fría cut-off lows form when warm Mediterranean moisture meets cold upper-air pools — a recurring autumn pattern affecting Spanish-Mediterranean coasts including the Balearics and Costa Blanca.
